column2: Jarvis is the human-agent collaboration and learning-loop protocol. It defines WorkSession, Policy, Request, Review, Takeover, Contribution, EvidenceManifest, LearningRecord, MemoryProposal, and SkillProposal so existing agents and host products can collaborate with humans without replacing their runtimes.

Near-term priority: prove one v0 protocol loop end to end: protocol lock, schemas, version and capability negotiation, adapter contract, Garden POC mapping, portable export, and conformance fixtures. Garden POC is the product proof before main Garden adopts the protocol. Keep scope narrow: protocol records first, Garden POC plus one real existing-agent adapter, evidence and learning captured during work.

column3: Turn Jarvis into an implementation-ready protocol lock.
column4:
- freeze v0 protocol lock
- align README, principles, architecture, roadmap
- keep language direct and host-generic
- confirm object names and lifecycle
column5:
- define schema entry gate
- list open schema questions
- identify version, capability, extension, and error-model gaps
- prepare schema folder layout
column6:
- name first adapter targets
- define what an existing agent must expose
- map agent steps to JarvisEvent
- map blocked action to Request
column7:
- choose Garden POC as the first product proof
- choose one real existing-agent adapter
- define Garden-private fields that stay out of export
- define demo success path
column8: Protocol lock is the source for schema work, Garden POC and adapter targets are named, and no new runtime/product assumptions enter Jarvis.

column4:
- finalize required object meanings
- lock WorkSession state transitions
- lock Request, Review, Takeover states
- lock Contribution and Evidence terms
column5:
- create JSON schemas for core objects
- create JarvisEvent envelope schema
- create portable export schema
- define protocol error codes
column6:
- draft adapter contract
- define AgentWorker adapter surface
- define tool/action to PolicyDecision mapping
- define limitations field for unsupported concepts
column7:
- create Garden POC mapping table
- define Garden WorkSession flow
- define Request inbox behavior
- define Review and Takeover capture points
column8: A developer can read the schemas and know exactly how to create a valid WorkSession, Request, Review, Contribution, EvidenceManifest, and LearningRecord.

column3: Make conformance strong enough to prove compatibility.
column4:
- write compatibility rules
- define extension namespace rules
- define version negotiation
- define capability negotiation
column5:
- build golden-path fixtures
- build failing fixtures
- validate status transitions
- validate export does not require Garden-private fields
column6:
- create CLI/existing-agent adapter plan
- capture messages/actions as JarvisEvents
- capture tool use as PolicyDecision and Evidence
- capture human correction as Review
column7:
- run Garden POC mapping against schemas
- generate sample WorkSession export
- show evidence and learning records
- document host-only assumptions separately
column8: Conformance rejects missing policy, missing review, stale takeover continuation, unattributed contribution, weak evidence, and silent learning mutation.

column3: Prove Jarvis with Garden POC and one existing-agent adapter.
column4:
- refine protocol from real fixture pain
- keep object names stable
- document compatibility decisions
- update protocol lock only for real contradictions
column5:
- run schemas against host export
- run schemas against CLI/adapter export
- compare both exports for same core semantics
- record gaps as conformance issues
column6:
- implement proof adapter path
- HumanWorker objective -> AgentWorker action
- blocked action -> Request
- human response -> Review or Takeover
column7:
- demonstrate Garden POC flow
- export EvidenceManifest
- export LearningRecord
- show human, agent, and pair learning
- update simulation narrative if needed
column8: Two different host or adapter shapes produce valid Jarvis records for the same collaboration loop without sharing runtime, UI, storage, or execution stack.

column3: Freeze v0.1 alpha and prepare team/public review.
column4:
- close protocol lock gaps
- publish compatibility notes
- summarize decisions and non-goals
- prepare v0.2 evidence/learning backlog
column5:
- freeze v0.1 schema draft
- publish conformance checklist
- publish valid/invalid examples
- define migration policy draft
column6:
- write adapter guide
- document CLI/existing-agent adapter
- document host implementation guide
- identify next two adapter targets
column7:
- publish final demo URL
- package Garden POC evidence
- prepare team walkthrough
- collect review notes into next-cycle issues
column8: Jarvis has a v0.1 alpha package of protocol docs, schemas, examples, conformance fixtures, Garden POC proof, and one existing-agent adapter proof.

column3: Expand only from conformance and proof-host evidence.
column4:
- harden object model from real adapters
- add migration rules
- add public implementation checklist
- prepare v0.2 evidence and learning beta
column5:
- richer EvidenceManifest
- stronger Contribution taxonomy
- review-state workflows
- memory and skill compatibility rules
column6:
- more agent adapters
- MCP/A2A/AG-UI integration notes
- SDK-neutral adapter examples
- compatibility badges
column7:
- additional product proofs
- public demo refresh
- team onboarding sheet
- protocol review sessions
column8: Expansion is justified by valid exports, conformance failures, adapter pain, and Garden POC evidence, not by adding features from vibes.
